incidents.title: Phishing Email Targeting Local Bank Customers
incidents.description: Multiple residents in Downtown have reported receiving emails impersonating State Bank of India (SBI) asking them to verify their accounts. The emails contain links to a fake SBI login page that harvests credentials. The domain used is sbi-secure-update.com which is not an official SBI domain.

incidents.title: Vehicle Break-ins Reported Near Riverside Park
incidents.description: Three vehicles were broken into overnight in the Riverside Park parking area. Items stolen include laptops, bags, and electronics left visible in the cars. Local police have increased patrols in the area. Residents are advised to avoid leaving valuables in their vehicles.

incidents.title: Tech Support Scam Calls Targeting Elderly Residents
incidents.description: Several elderly residents in Oakwood have reported receiving calls from individuals claiming to be from Microsoft or Apple tech support. The callers request remote access to the victim's computer and charge fees for unnecessary repairs. One resident lost approximately $500 before realizing it was a scam.

incidents.title: Major Pothole on Hilltop Main Road
incidents.description: A large pothole has formed on the main road near Hilltop Community Center. The pothole is approximately 2 feet wide and 6 inches deep, posing a risk to vehicles and cyclists. Local authorities have been notified but repairs may take several days.

incidents.title: Ransomware Campaign Targeting Home Networks
incidents.description: A new ransomware variant called 'HomeLocker' is spreading through infected email attachments and compromised websites. It specifically targets home routers and IoT devices. Once infected, all connected devices may have their files encrypted. Security researchers recommend updating router firmware and enabling network firewalls immediately.

incidents.title: Fake Charity Door-to-Door Solicitation
incidents.description: Individuals posing as charity workers have been going door-to-door in Greenfield, claiming to collect donations for a children's hospital. They carry fake ID badges and printed materials. The hospital has confirmed they have no such campaign. Residents should not provide cash or personal information to these individuals.

incidents.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning for Riverside Area
incidents.description: The meteorological department has issued a severe thunderstorm warning for the Riverside area. Heavy rainfall with possible hail and strong winds of up to 60 mph are expected between 4 PM and 10 PM. Flooding in low-lying areas is possible. Residents should secure outdoor items and avoid unnecessary travel.

incidents.title: Suspicious Person Reported Near Oakwood Elementary School
incidents.description: A community member reported a suspicious individual loitering near Oakwood Elementary School during morning drop-off hours. The individual was described as wearing a dark hoodie and appeared to be photographing children. School security has been notified, and local police are increasing patrols around the school.

incidents.title: Water Quality Advisory for Downtown Area
incidents.description: The water utility has issued an advisory for the Downtown area due to elevated levels of chlorine detected during routine testing. While the water is still safe for bathing, residents are advised to use bottled water for drinking and cooking until further notice. The issue is expected to be resolved within 48 hours.

incidents.title: Wi-Fi Network Spoofing at Lakeview Coffee Shops
incidents.description: Security researchers have identified fake Wi-Fi hotspots set up near popular coffee shops in Lakeview. These hotspots use names like 'Lakeview_Cafe_Free_WiFi' and 'CoffeeShop_Guest' to trick users into connecting. Once connected, attackers can intercept passwords and sensitive data. Use VPN when on public Wi-Fi.

incidents.title: Gas Leak Reported on Hilltop Commercial Street
incidents.description: A natural gas leak has been detected near 45 Commercial Street in Hilltop. The fire department has cordoned off a 200-meter radius around the site. Nearby businesses have been temporarily evacuated. Residents are advised to stay away from the area until the all-clear is given.

incidents.title: Package Theft Ring Operating in Greenfield
incidents.description: Multiple package thefts have been reported in the Greenfield residential area over the past two weeks. Security cameras have captured what appears to be a coordinated group following delivery trucks and stealing packages within minutes of delivery. Police recommend using package lockers or requiring signatures for deliveries.

incidents.title: UPI Payment Scam via Fake QR Codes
incidents.description: Fraudsters are placing fake QR code stickers over legitimate ones at small shops and parking meters in Downtown. When scanned, these codes direct payments to the scammer's account instead of the intended recipient. Several small business owners and customers have been affected. Always verify the payment recipient name before confirming.

incidents.title: Power Outage Expected During Grid Maintenance
incidents.description: The electric utility company has scheduled grid maintenance for the Oakwood area. A planned power outage is expected on March 20 from 10 AM to 4 PM. Residents should charge essential devices beforehand and ensure backup for medical equipment. The utility will provide updates via SMS.

incidents.title: SIM Swapping Attacks Targeting Local Residents
incidents.description: Several residents in Riverside have fallen victim to SIM swapping attacks where criminals contact the mobile carrier pretending to be the account holder and transfer the phone number to a new SIM. This gives them access to SMS-based 2FA codes and financial accounts. Contact your carrier to set up a SIM PIN immediately.

incidents.title: Flu Outbreak at Greenfield Community Center
incidents.description: The local health department has reported a cluster of influenza cases linked to events at the Greenfield Community Center. Over 30 attendees of a recent gathering have reported symptoms. The center has been temporarily closed for deep cleaning. Residents who attended recent events should monitor for symptoms and consult a doctor if needed.

incidents.title: Fallen Tree Blocking Lakeview Bicycle Path
incidents.description: A large oak tree has fallen across the main bicycle path near Lakeview Lake, completely blocking passage. The tree also brought down a small section of fencing. Parks department has been notified. Cyclists and pedestrians should use the alternate route via Pine Street until the path is cleared.

incidents.title: Rental Scam on Social Media Marketplace
incidents.description: Fraudulent rental listings have appeared on social media marketplace for properties in Hilltop that are either not for rent or don't exist. Scammers are asking for security deposits and first month's rent via wire transfer. At least three families have lost deposits ranging from $1,000 to $2,500. Always verify listings through official rental platforms.

incidents.title: Ice Storm Warning for Eastern Neighborhoods
incidents.description: An ice storm warning has been issued for eastern neighborhoods including Oakwood and Hilltop. Freezing rain is expected to produce ice accumulations of up to half an inch. Road conditions will be hazardous. Schools in the affected areas may announce closures. Stock up on essentials and avoid driving.

incidents.title: Vandalism at Riverside Community Garden
incidents.description: The Riverside Community Garden was vandalized overnight. Several raised beds were overturned, tools were stolen from the shed, and graffiti was sprayed on the garden walls. The community garden committee has filed a police report and is seeking any witnesses or security footage from neighboring properties.

incidents.title: Data Breach at Local Healthcare Provider
incidents.description: Hilltop Medical Associates has reported a data breach that may have exposed patient records including names, dates of birth, and insurance information. The breach is believed to have occurred through a compromised employee email account. Affected patients will be notified by mail. If you are a patient, monitor your credit reports and enable fraud alerts.

incidents.title: Construction Noise Complaint - Night Work on Main Street
incidents.description: Residents near Downtown Main Street are complaining about construction noise during late night hours. The ongoing building project has been operating heavy machinery between 11 PM and 5 AM, violating the local noise ordinance. Multiple complaints have been filed with the city council.

incidents.title: Lottery Prize Notification Email Scam
incidents.description: Residents across multiple neighborhoods have reported receiving emails claiming they've won a large lottery prize from an international lottery they never entered. The emails request personal information and a processing fee to claim winnings. This is a classic advance-fee fraud. Delete these emails immediately.

incidents.title: Suspicious Drone Activity Over Greenfield Residential Area
incidents.description: Multiple residents in Greenfield have reported seeing an unidentified drone flying over their properties during evening hours. The drone appears to have a camera attached and has been spotted on three consecutive nights. While recreational drone use is legal, flying over private property raises privacy concerns. FAA and local police have been notified.

incidents.title: Food Recall: Contaminated Salad Mix at Local Grocery
incidents.description: The FDA has issued a recall for 'Fresh Garden Salad Mix' sold at GreenMart stores in Downtown and Riverside due to potential E. coli contamination. The affected products have lot numbers FM-2026-0312 through FM-2026-0316. If you purchased this product, do not consume it and return to the store for a refund.

incidents.title: Credential Stuffing Attacks on Local Services
incidents.description: Multiple local online services including the library portal, community center booking system, and neighborhood forum have reported credential stuffing attacks. Hackers are using leaked password databases from other breaches to try logging into these local accounts. If you reuse passwords across services, change them immediately and use unique passwords.

incidents.title: Heavy Fog Advisory for Lakeview and Riverside
incidents.description: Dense fog is expected in the Lakeview and Riverside areas through early morning hours. Visibility may drop below 200 meters. Drivers should use low-beam headlights, reduce speed, and maintain extra following distance. The fog is expected to lift by 10 AM.

incidents.title: Someone almost ran me over ugh terrible drivers in this area
incidents.description: I was crossing the street near the Hilltop mall and this car just zoomed past me so close I could feel the wind. These drivers need to learn how to drive properly. I'm so sick of this happening every single day. The traffic lights are useless and nobody follows the rules anymore.

incidents.title: Why is the internet so slow today??
incidents.description: My internet has been super slow all day and I cant even stream properly. Is anyone else having this issue in Oakwood? I already called my ISP and they said nothing is wrong on their end but it's clearly broken. Maybe there's some kind of outage they're not telling us about?

incidents.title: Dog left barking all night at Greenfield Apartments
incidents.description: For the third night in a row, a dog at the Greenfield Apartments building C has been barking non-stop from midnight until early morning. Multiple residents have complained to the building management but nothing has been done. This is affecting everyone's sleep and quality of life in the building.

incidents.title: Broken Street Light at Riverside and Oak Junction
incidents.description: The street light at the junction of Riverside Road and Oak Lane has been out for over a week now. The intersection becomes very dark after sunset, which is a safety hazard for both pedestrians and vehicles. The city maintenance department has been contacted but no repair has been made yet.

incidents.title: Air Quality Alert: Smoke from Nearby Agricultural Burning
incidents.description: Air quality in Hilltop and surrounding areas has deteriorated due to agricultural burning in rural areas east of the city. The AQI has reached 175 (Unhealthy). Residents with respiratory conditions should stay indoors and keep windows closed. The situation is expected to improve in 2-3 days as wind patterns shift.

incidents.title: Fake Amazon Delivery OTP Scam
incidents.description: Residents in Lakeview are reporting a new scam where callers claim to be from Amazon delivery service and ask for an OTP (one-time password) to deliver a package. This OTP is actually for unauthorized transactions on the victim's account. Amazon never calls customers for OTPs. Hang up immediately if you receive such a call.

incidents.title: Bluetooth Skimming Devices Found at Greenfield ATMs
incidents.description: Police have discovered Bluetooth-enabled card skimming devices installed on two ATMs near Greenfield Shopping Center. These devices capture card data and PINs when customers use the ATMs. Two devices have been removed so far, but others may remain. Always cover the keypad when entering your PIN and check for loose components on the card slot.
